---
title: "Modeling Song Popularity"
author: "Khrystyna Vinichuk"
format: 
  revealjs:
    theme: dark
    slide-number: true
---

## Problem

What makes a song popular?

- Energy  
- Danceability  
- Tempo  

Understanding these factors can help predict trends in music and explain listener preferences.

---

## Data

Dataset includes:

- Energy  
- Danceability  
- Tempo  
- Popularity  

The data was cleaned and selected to focus only on the most relevant variables for analysis.

---

## Energy vs Popularity

![](Visualization/Energy_vs_Popularity.png){fig-align="center"}

- Weak to moderate relationship  
- Slight positive trend  

Higher energy songs tend to attract attention, but energy alone does not guarantee success.

---

## Danceability vs Popularity

![](Visualization/Danceability_vs_Popularity.png){fig-align="center"}

- Stronger relationship  
- More danceable → more popular  

Danceability appears to be one of the key factors influencing listener engagement.

---

## Tempo vs Popularity

![](Visualization/Tempo_vs_Popularity.png){fig-align="center"}

- Weak relationship  
- Not a strong predictor  

Songs with very different tempos can still achieve similar levels of popularity.

---

## Correlation Matrix

![](Visualization/Correlation_Matrix.png){fig-align="center"}

- Danceability strongest  
- Tempo weakest  

This confirms that not all features contribute equally to popularity.

---

## Model

Linear Regression:

$$
Popularity = a + b1·Energy + b2·Danceability + b3·Tempo
$$  

The model estimates how each feature contributes to the final popularity score.

---

## Actual vs Predicted

![](Visualization/Actual_vs_Predicted.png){fig-align="center"}

- Model captures trend  
- Not perfectly accurate  

Predictions follow the general pattern, but there is still noticeable deviation.

---

## Limitations

- Simple model  
- Other factors matter:
  - Trends  
  - Marketing  
  - Artist popularity  

The model does not include social or cultural influences, which can be very important.

---

## Conclusion

- Danceability matters most  
- Tempo matters least  
- Model gives reasonable prediction  

Even though the model is simple, it provides useful insights into what drives song popularity.

## Sources

The following resources were used during the creation of this project:

- [Spotify Dataset (2010–2019)](https://github.com/Demibolt007/Spotify-Streaming-Insights-2010-2019/blob/main/Spotify%20Dataset.csv)  
  Used as the main dataset for analyzing relationships between song features and popularity.

- [Scikit-learn Documentation](https://scikit-learn.org/stable/)  
  Used for implementing the linear regression model and understanding machine learning functions.

- [Spotify Streaming Insights Repository](https://github.com/Demibolt007/Spotify-Streaming-Insights-2010-2019)  
  Used as an additional reference for understanding the dataset structure and project context.
